name: Smart Contracts
description: Plutus platform, on-chain languages, EUTxO model, patterns, protocols, tooling, and standards.

tours.title: EUTxO Fundamentals
tours.description: The transaction model that makes Cardano smart contracts work.

stops.title: The Extended UTxO Model
stops.narrative: Cardano extends Bitcoin's UTxO model with three key additions: [datums](node:datum) for state, [redeemers](node:redeemer) for user actions, and [script context](node:script-context) for transaction introspection. This makes UTxOs programmable while keeping transactions deterministic — you know the outcome before submitting.

stops.title: Datums: Contract State
stops.narrative: A [datum](node:datum) is data attached to a UTxO locked by a script. It represents the contract's state. With [inline datums](node:inline-datums) (CIP-32), the full datum lives on-chain — anyone can read the contract state by querying the UTxO.

stops.title: On-Chain Execution
stops.narrative: [Plutus Core](node:plutus-core) is the low-level lambda calculus that runs on-chain. The [CEK machine](node:cek-machine) evaluates it, tracking [ExUnits](node:exunits) (CPU + memory) against the transaction budget. Exceeding the budget = transaction fails.

stops.title: What Scripts Cost
stops.narrative: [Cost models](node:cost-models) assign costs to each Plutus built-in function. These are [protocol parameters](node:param-group-technical) that governance can update via [parameter change actions](node:action-param-change). ExUnits are converted to ada fees using price parameters.

tours.title: Smart Contract Languages
tours.description: 8 languages that compile to Plutus Core — pick the one that fits your team.

stops.title: Aiken: The Popular Choice
stops.narrative: [Aiken](node:aiken) is used by 75%+ of Cardano developers. Rust-inspired syntax, purpose-built for smart contracts. Compiles to [UPLC](node:plutus-core) with aggressive optimization. Auto-generates [CIP-57](node:cip-57) blueprints. [Minswap](node:minswap) V2 is written in Aiken.

stops.title: Plinth: The Haskell Original
stops.narrative: [Plinth](node:plinth) (formerly PlutusTx) is the original on-chain language. Write in a subset of Haskell, get the full GHC type system. Maintained by IntersectMBO. [Plutarch](node:plutarch) offers a lower-level Haskell eDSL for performance-critical validators — used by [Liqwid](node:liqwid).

stops.title: OpShin: Python for Cardano
stops.narrative: [OpShin](node:opshin) lets you write validators in 100% valid Python syntax. Strict typing enforced at compile time. Integrates with PyCardano for off-chain code. The lowest barrier to entry for Python developers.

stops.title: Marlowe: Financial Contracts
stops.narrative: [Marlowe](node:marlowe) is a DSL for financial contracts — non-Turing-complete by design, so contracts can be exhaustively analyzed and formally verified. Think loans, escrows, and swaps with mathematical guarantees. Now community-owned.

stops.title: The JS/TS Options
stops.narrative: [Helios](node:helios) and [plu-ts](node:plu-ts) target JavaScript/TypeScript developers. Helios has its own on-chain DSL plus off-chain tx building. plu-ts constructs UPLC AST directly in TypeScript. [Scalus](node:scalus) covers Scala developers.

tours.title: Vasil / Babbage Features
tours.description: CIP-31, CIP-32, CIP-33 — the upgrades that made smart contracts practical.

stops.title: Reference Scripts (CIP-33)
stops.narrative: [Reference scripts](node:reference-scripts) store scripts on-chain in a UTxO. Transactions reference them instead of including the script body. Dramatically reduces fees — a popular contract is deployed once and reused by everyone.

stops.title: Reference Inputs (CIP-31)
stops.narrative: [Reference inputs](node:reference-inputs) let transactions read UTxOs without spending them. This enables the [oracle pattern](node:oracle-pattern): publish price data once, read it from many contracts simultaneously without contention.

stops.title: Inline Datums (CIP-32)
stops.narrative: [Inline datums](node:inline-datums) store the full [datum](node:datum) directly in the UTxO. Before CIP-32, you had to manage datum hashes off-chain. Now contract state is visible to everyone on-chain — essential for composability.

tours.title: Smart Contract Patterns
tours.description: Common design patterns for building on EUTxO.

stops.title: State Machines
stops.narrative: The [state machine pattern](node:state-machine) models contract state as a [datum](node:datum). The validator checks that input datum + redeemer produces a valid output datum. Used for multi-step protocols: escrow, auctions, DEX order matching.

stops.title: Oracle Pattern
stops.narrative: The [oracle pattern](node:oracle-pattern) uses [reference inputs](node:reference-inputs) to publish data that many contracts can read without spending. Price feeds, exchange rates, random numbers — all published as UTxO datums.

stops.title: Withdraw Zero Trick
stops.narrative: The [withdraw zero pattern](node:staking-validator) is a key optimization. Instead of running a spending validator per input (O(n²) cost), delegate validation to a staking validator that runs once per transaction (O(n)). Documented by Anastasia Labs.

stops.title: Minting Policies & Token Standards
stops.narrative: [Minting policies](node:minting-policy) control token creation. [CIP-25](node:cip-25) defines immutable NFT metadata at mint time. [CIP-68](node:cip-68) adds mutable metadata via reference tokens — the standard for modern Cardano NFTs and rich fungible tokens.

tours.title: Protocols & Tooling
tours.description: What people are building and the tools they use.

stops.title: DEX: Minswap
stops.narrative: [Minswap](node:minswap) is the largest Cardano DEX by TVL. Multi-pool AMM written in [Aiken](node:aiken). Batchers collect user swap orders and settle them against pool UTxOs — the standard DEX architecture on EUTxO.

stops.title: Lending: Liqwid Finance
stops.narrative: [Liqwid](node:liqwid) is the leading lending/borrowing protocol. Uses [Plutarch](node:plutarch) for performance-critical validators. DAO-governed. Demonstrates complex multi-contract DeFi on [EUTxO](node:eutxo).

stops.title: Scaling: Hydra L2
stops.narrative: [Hydra](node:hydra) is Cardano's Layer 2 — isomorphic state channels that [replicate the full EUTxO model](node:eutxo) off-chain. Sub-second latency, 1,000 TPS per head. Same smart contract model as L1, so existing contracts work.

stops.title: Off-Chain: Lucid & Mesh
stops.narrative: [Lucid Evolution](node:lucid-evolution) and [Mesh SDK](node:mesh-sdk) are the main TypeScript libraries for building transactions that interact with [Plutus](node:plutus) validators. [Blockfrost](node:blockfrost) provides chain indexing APIs. [Demeter](node:demeter) offers cloud infrastructure.
